content_block.content: **Expert‑curated 7‑day Vietnam itinerary** (4–10 Apr 2026) focusing on **culture, handicrafts, local life, natural exploration, and history**. This plan follows a **Hong Kong → Da Nang → Hue → Hoi An → Ho Chi Minh City → Hong Kong** route with no backtracking, maximizing swimming opportunities and craft‑village visits.
🗺️ Route Choice Comparison

**Option A (recommended):** Hong Kong → Da Nang → Hue → Hoi An → Ho Chi Minh City → Hong Kong
• **No backtracking** – one‑way flow southward
• **1 domestic flight** (Da Nang → HCMC)
• **Lower cost** (fewer flights)
• **Better energy distribution** – front‑loaded with beach/activity, ending with city exploration

**Option B:** Hong Kong → Ho Chi Minh City → Da Nang → Hue → Hoi An → HCMC → Hong Kong
• **Requires backtracking** – extra flight back to HCMC
• **2 domestic flights** (HCMC→Da Nang, Da Nang→HCMC)
• **Higher cost & more travel fatigue**
✈️ Flight Arrangements (4–10 April 2026)

**Outbound:** Hong Kong → Da Nang (4 Apr)
• **UO552** (08:25–09:20) – recommended (arrive early, full day)
• **GBA** (07:55–09:00) – slightly cheaper
• **UO558** (16:50–17:45) – afternoon departure

**Domestic:** Da Nang → Ho Chi Minh City (8 or 9 Apr)
• **Vietnam Airlines** (1.5 h, ~800,000–1,200,000 VND)
• **VietJet Air** (1.5 h, ~600,000–1,000,000 VND)
• Flights abundant throughout the day

**Return:** Ho Chi Minh City → Hong Kong (10 Apr)
• **VJ876** (15:10–18:50) – recommended
• Many other options (53 daily flights)
📅 7‑Day Detailed Itinerary (4–10 Apr)
**Day 1 (4 Apr, Sat) – Arrival in Da Nang & My Khe Beach**

• **08:25** – Fly HKG→DAD (UO552)
• **09:20** – Arrive Da Nang; e‑visa entry; Grab to hotel
• **Afternoon** – Check‑in at My Khe Beach hotel; lunch at seafood restaurant
• **Late afternoon** – First swim at My Khe Beach (water ~28 °C)
• **Evening** – Beach sunset; Dragon Bridge fire/water show (weekends, 21:00)
**Day 2 (5 Apr, Sun) – Da Nang: Marble Mountains & Stone‑Carving Village**

• **Morning** – Swim at My Khe Beach (06:00–07:30)
• **Late morning** – Marble Mountains (Thuy Son) – cave temples, elevator to summit
• **Afternoon** – Non Nuoc Stone‑Carving Village – 300 workshops, marble souvenirs (Buddha statues, zodiac figurines)
• **Late afternoon** – Da Nang Cathedral (Pink Church) photo stop
• **Evening** – Vietnamese seafood dinner along My Khe Beach
**Day 3 (6 Apr, Mon) – Da Nang → Hoi An**

• **Morning** – Check‑out, Grab to Hoi An (~40 min, ~350,000 VND)
• **Afternoon** – Check‑in near Ancient Town; Thanh Ha Pottery Village – hand‑make couple‑mugs
• **Evening** – Swim at An Bang Beach (quieter than My Khe)
• **Night** – Hoi An lantern evening + floating‑lantern release
**Day 4 (7 Apr, Tue) – Hoi An Craft‑Focused Day**

• **Morning** – Swim at An Bang Beach; explore Hoi An Ancient Town craft street (Japanese Bridge, Fujian Assembly Hall)
• **Lunch** – Must‑try Cao Lau + White Rose dumplings
• **Afternoon** – Lantern‑making workshop (take‑home silk lantern)
• **Evening** – Swim at Chu Dai Beach (10 min from town)
• **Night** – Night market shopping & street food
**Day 5 (8 Apr, Wed) – Hoi An → Hue**

• **Morning** – Free time in Hoi An / optional swim at Chu Dai Beach
• **Afternoon** – Shared bus/Grab to Hue (2.5–3 h) via Hai Van Pass (National Geographic “50 Places of a Lifetime”)
• **Late afternoon** – Check‑in near Perfume River; stroll around Imperial Citadel moat
• **Evening** – Imperial‑style dinner
**Day 6 (9 Apr, Thu) – Hue Deep Dive → Fly to Ho Chi Minh City**

• **Morning** – Hue Imperial City (UNESCO) – Forbidden Purple City, Ngo Mon Gate, Thai Hoa Palace
• **Afternoon** – Royal Tombs (Minh Mang, Tu Duc) + Thien Mu Pagoda
• **Late afternoon** – Transfer to Da Nang Airport (2 h)
• **Evening** – Fly DAD→SGN (~1.5 h); check‑in District 1
**Day 7 (10 Apr, Fri) – Ho Chi Minh City (French‑Colonial Highlights)**

• **Morning** – Central Post Office + Notre‑Dame Cathedral (exterior)
• **Afternoon** – Reunification Palace + War Remnants Museum
• **Evening** – Ben Thanh Market – handicrafts, coffee, cashews
• **Night** – Bui Vien Street for Saigon beer
**⚠️ Safety note:** Ho Chi Minh City has high motorcycle density; keep phones/purses secure, cross streets steadily.
**Day 8 (11 Apr, Sat) – Departure**

• **Morning** – Hotel breakfast, last‑minute souvenir shopping
• **Afternoon** – Check‑out, transfer to airport
• **15:10** – Fly SGN→HKG (VJ876)
• **18:50** – Arrive Hong Kong
🏊 Swimming Opportunities

• **4 Apr (evening)** – My Khe Beach (first dip + sunset)
• **5 Apr (morning)** – My Khe Beach (06:00–07:30)
• **6 Apr (evening)** – An Bang Beach (quieter)
• **7 Apr (morning & evening)** – An Bang + Chu Dai Beach (main swimming day)
• **8 Apr (optional)** – Chu Dai Beach before departure to Hue

**Total:** 4–5 swims, comfortable pace.
🛍️ Handicraft Shopping List

• **Da Nang (Non Nuoc)** – Marble Buddha statues, zodiac figurines
• **Hoi An (Ancient Town)** – Silk lanterns, custom Áo Dài, wood carvings, lacquerware
• **Hoi An (Thanh Ha)** – Hand‑made pottery (couple mugs)
• **Hue (Imperial City)** – Court‑style souvenirs
• **Ho Chi Minh City (Ben Thanh Market)** – Silk, lacquerware, coffee, cashews
⚠️ Important Reminders

• **Visa** – E‑visa (USD 25, 3–5 working days); select Da Nang (DAD) as entry, Ho Chi Minh (SGN) as exit; carry printed copy.
• **Safety** – Keep phones/bags secure in HCMC; use Grab (app‑based) for taxis; avoid casinos (recent warnings).
• **Emergency contacts** – Vietnam police 113; Consulate in Da Nang +84‑905‑580‑010; Consulate in HCMC +84‑908‑002‑226; Chinese MFA +86‑10‑12308.
📋 Immediate Next Steps

1. Apply for e‑visa (today/tomorrow)
2. Book HKG→DAD flight (UO552 recommended)
3. Book SGN→HKG return flight
4. Domestic DAD→SGN flight can be booked later (plenty of availability)
5. After flights confirmed, refine hotel & restaurant list.
